content:
🌟 Lemuroid | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Emulating classic video game consoles on Android devices.
✨ Key Features: • Libretro integration • Wide system support (Atari, Nintendo, Sega, PlayStation, PSP, NDS, 3DS, Arcade, etc.) • Ease of use and good Android integration • Automatic save/restore game states • ROM scanning and indexing
📖 Summary: Lemuroid is an open-source Android emulator based on Libretro, designed for ease of use and a great user experience. It supports a wide range of classic gaming consoles and offers features like automatic state saving, ROM scanning, and customizable touch controls.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 undiscord | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Bulk deletion of messages in Discord channels or direct messages via a browser extension.
✨ Key Features: • Bulk delete all messages in a Discord channel or DM • Operates as a browser extension/userscript • Easy and fast message deletion • Requires browser-based Discord access (not the desktop app)
📖 Summary: Undiscord is a browser extension and userscript designed to efficiently delete all messages within a Discord channel or direct message. It operates directly within the browser, offering a fast and easy way to manage message history.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 android-sms-gateway | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling programmatic sending and receiving of SMS messages through an API, integrating SMS functionality into other applications and services.
✨ Key Features: • Send and Receive SMS via API • Support for Android 5.0 and above • Multipart message support • Message status tracking • Incoming messages viewer
📖 Summary: The Android SMS Gateway app transforms an Android device into an SMS gateway, allowing users to send and receive SMS messages programmatically via an API. It supports direct device access or cloud server integration, making it suitable for embedding SMS functionality into other applications.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 agenticSeek | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a private, autonomous AI assistant that can perform web browsing, coding, and task management locally on a user's machine, without relying on cloud services.
✨ Key Features: • Fully Local & Private operation • Smart Web Browsing capabilities • Autonomous Coding Assistant for multiple languages • Automatic Agent Selection for tasks • Complex Task Planning and Execution
📖 Summary: AgenticSeek is a fully local, voice-enabled AI assistant that autonomously browses the web, writes code, and plans complex tasks. It functions entirely on the user's hardware, ensuring complete privacy and eliminating cloud dependency, making it a private alternative to services like Manus AI.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ editor | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating and sharing 3D architectural projects.
✨ Key Features: • 3D architectural project creation and sharing • Built with React Three Fiber and WebGPU • Turborepo monorepo architecture • Separation of concerns into core, viewer, and editor packages • Zustand stores for scene, viewer, and editor state management
📖 Summary: Pascal Editor is a 3D architectural building editor built using React Three Fiber and WebGPU, leveraging a monorepo architecture with Turborepo. It separates concerns into core schema and state management, 3D rendering components, and the main editor application, enabling the creation and sharing of architectural projects.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ MedgeClaw | TeX
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a conversational AI interface for executing complex biomedical research analyses, making advanced bioinformatics and drug discovery workflows accessible through natural language interaction.
✨ Key Features: • AI-powered biomedical research assistant • Supports RNA-seq, drug discovery, clinical analysis, and more • Integrates 140 K-Dense Scientific Skills • Real-time research dashboard
📖 Summary: MedgeClaw is an open-source AI research assistant designed for biomedical applications. It allows users to interact via messaging apps like WhatsApp or Slack to initiate complex analyses such as RNA-seq, drug discovery, and clinical research, with results accessible through RStudio or JupyterLab.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
